title: Robots in Our World
passage:
Robots in Our Lives

Robots are smart machines. They can do many different things to help humans. Today, we can find robots in our homes, in big hospitals, and in schools. Some robots are very simple. Other robots use Artificial Intelligence, or AI. AI helps robots think, learn, and make decisions.

In many homes, people use small cleaning robots. These robots are usually round and flat. They move around the floor and clean dust and dirt. They have small computers and sensors inside them. Sensors help the robot see walls, tables, and chairs so they do not hit them. Many people buy these robots because they save a lot of time every day.

In factories, robots are also very important. They do difficult and dangerous jobs. For example, they can lift heavy boxes or paint cars. They do not get tired, and they can work all day and night. This helps companies make things very fast and cheap.

In hospitals, doctors use special robots. These robots are very precise and careful. They help doctors during difficult operations. There are also small delivery robots in hospitals. These robots carry hot food and medicine to different rooms. This helps nurses because they do not have to carry heavy things, and they can spend more time with patients.

In schools, students use toy robots to learn. They learn how to program these toys. Programming means giving simple instructions to the robot. For example, a student can program a robot to walk forward, turn left, or make a happy sound. This is a very fun way for young children to learn computer science.

In the future, we will have many more robots. Some scientists are making robots that look like real humans. These are called humanoid robots. They can talk, smile, and help old people with their daily activities. Robots will make our lives easier, but they can never replace real human friends.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'Other robots use Artificial Intelligence, or AI. AI helps robots think, learn, and make decisions.'

options.A: Giving simple instructions to a robot

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Cleaning robots use sensors to avoid hitting walls.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Sensors help the robot see walls, tables, and chairs so they do not hit them.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Factory robots only work during the day.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'They do not get tired, and they can work all day and night.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Delivery robots in hospitals carry food and medicine.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'These robots carry hot food and medicine to different rooms.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Humanoid robots are very expensive to buy.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this

questions.explanation: The passage mentions humanoid robots in the last paragraph, but it does not mention how much they cost.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Robots in factories do jobs that are difficult and ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'They do difficult and dangerous jobs.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 6 states: 'Some scientists are making robots that look like real humans.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Students in schools use ______ robots to learn computer science.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'In schools, students use toy robots to learn.'

vocabulary.meaning: Intelligent; able to think quickly and learn easily.
Small devices that help a machine feel or see things around it.
To give instructions to a computer or a machine to make it do something.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi thường dùng từ vựng giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ khóa như 'sensors', 'factory', 'toy robots' trong bài để tìm ra đoạn văn chứa đáp án nhanh nhất.

2. Cách làm dạng bài True/False/Not Given:
- Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi khớp hoàn toàn với bài đọc -> Chọn TRUE.
- Nếu thông tin ngược lại hoàn toàn (ví dụ: bài viết nói 'work all day and night' nhưng câu hỏi nói 'only work during the day') -> Chọn FALSE.
- Nếu thông tin không được nhắc tới (ví dụ: giá tiền của robot) -> Chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Lưu ý giới hạn từ: Đối với dạng bài điền từ (Sentence Completion), hãy chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS'. Chỉ điền từ chính xác lấy ra từ bài đọc, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ.
